author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, a psalm of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title is a familiar one; the Psalm is of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, the King shall rejoice in Thy strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, in Thy strength, whereby the Word was made flesh, the Man Christ Jesus shall rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And shall exult exceedingly in Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in that, whereby Thou quickenest all things, shall exult exceedingly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast given Him the desire of His soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He desired to eat the Passover, and to lay down His life when He would, and again when He would to take it; and Thou hast given it to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hast not deprived Him of the good pleasure of His lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My peace,” saith He, “I leave with you:” and it was done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast presented Him with the blessings of sweetness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He had first quaffed the blessing of Thy sweetness, the gall of our sins did not hurt Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast set a crown of precious stone on His Head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At the beginning of His discoursing precious stones were brought, and compassed Him about; His disciples, from whom the commencement of His preaching should be made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He asked life; and Thou gavest Him:” He asked a resurrection, saying, “Father, glorify Thy Son;” and Thou gavest it Him, “Length of days for ever and ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The prolonged ages of this world which the Church was to have, and after them an eternity, world without end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His glory is great in Thy salvation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great indeed is His glory in the salvation, whereby Thou hast raised Him up again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Glory and great honour shalt Thou lay upon Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Thou shalt yet add unto Him glory and great honour, when Thou shalt place Him in heaven at Thy right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou shalt give Him blessing for ever and ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the blessing which Thou shalt give Him for ever and ever: “Thou shalt make Him glad in joy together with Thy countenance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: According to His manhood, Thou shalt make Him glad together with Thy countenance, which He lifted up to Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the King hopeth in the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the King is not proud, but humble in heart, he hopeth in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in the mercy of the Most Highest He shall not be moved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in the mercy of the Most Highest His obedience even unto the death of the Cross shall not disturb His humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Thy hand be found by all Thine enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be Thy power, O King, when Thou comest to judgment, found by all Thine enemies; who in Thy humiliation discerned it not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let Thy right hand find out all that hate Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the glory, wherein Thou reignest at the right hand of the Father, find out for punishment in the day of judgment all that hate Thee; for that now they have not found it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt make them like a fiery oven:” Thou shalt make them on fire within, by the consciousness of their ungodliness: “In the time of Thy countenance:” in the time of Thy manifestation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord shall trouble them in His wrath, and the fire shall devour them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then, being troubled by the vengeance of the Lord, after the accusation of their conscience, they shall be given up to eternal fire, to be devoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their fruit shalt Thou destroy out of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Their fruit, because it is earthly, shalt Thou destroy out of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And their seed from the sons of men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And their works; or, whomsoever they have seduced, Thou shalt not reckon among the sons of men, whom Thou hast called into the everlasting inheritance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because they turned evils against Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now this punishment shall be recompensed to them, because the evils which they supposed to hang over them by Thy reign, they turned against Thee to Thy death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They imagined a device, which they were not able to establish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They imagined a device, saying, “It is expedient that one die for all:” which they were not able to establish, not knowing what they said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou shalt set them low.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Thou shalt rank them among those from whom in degradation and contempt Thou wilt turn away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thy leavings Thou shalt make ready their countenance” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in these things that Thou leavest, that is, in the desires of an earthly kingdom, Thou shalt make ready their shamelessness for Thy passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be Thou exalted, O Lord, in Thy strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be Thou, Lord, whom in humiliation they did not discern, exalted in Thy strength, which they thought weakness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We will sing and praise Thy power.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In heart and in deed we will celebrate and make known Thy marvels.
